subject: Re: Comments on your Chapter 3 draft
body:
Dr. Lindström,

Attached is the full Chapter 3 draft (methods + results + a first pass at the discussion). I incorporated the Menge & Sutherland framing, standardized the site codes, and added the 2025 baseline to the warming-anomaly figure as a dashed line.

A couple of open questions I'd love your read on before the committee sees it:
- whether to fold Site 7 (Titlow Beach) into the outer-coast comparison or keep it separate given the swell exposure
- how much of the QA/QC workflow belongs in the methods vs. an appendix

Thank you — see you Friday.

Alex

subject: Inquiry — graduate research fellowship eligibility
body:
Dear Dr. Marsh,

I'm a second-year M.S. student in marine biology at the Puget Sound Institute of Science, working with Dr. Sarah Lindström on the effects of ocean warming on intertidal invertebrate communities in Puget Sound. We met briefly at the Salish Sea Research Cooperative field day last spring.

I'm exploring fellowship support for the analysis-and-writing phase of my thesis and the early stages of a follow-on project. Would you have a few minutes to point me toward whether the Cooperative's graduate fellowship is open to students at my stage, and the typical timeline?

Thank you for your time.

Best,
Alex Santos

subject: Committee meeting availability — March
body:
Dr. Hull,

I'm trying to lock in a committee meeting to walk through the warming-anomaly chapter before the department seminar. Are any of these windows workable for you: Tue Mar 17 afternoon, Thu Mar 19 morning, or Fri Mar 20 after 1pm?

I'll send the revised results section and the updated Site 7 figures a week ahead so there's time to read. Dr. Lindström is cc'd to coordinate her side.

Thank you,
Alex Santos

subject: Field gear checkout for Titlow Beach — Jan 24
body:
Hi Yolanda,

Can I reserve gear for a Titlow Beach (Site 7) run on Saturday Jan 24? I'd need the two 0.25 m2 quadrats, the YSI meter, a spare battery pack, and the waterproof field tablet.

Ben is coming to help with the transect counts, so we'll sign it all out under my name and have it back by Monday morning. Let me know if anything's already booked that day.

Thanks!
Alex

subject: Tacoma next weekend?
body:
Hey Kai,

I think next weekend works on my end — the good low tide is Saturday morning, so I'd be free from Saturday afternoon on. If you can catch the 2:40 train up, I can grab you from Tacoma Dome station.

Want to do the thing at the brewpub Saturday and something quiet Sunday? I've got the hard drive out so we can finally sort the camping photos. Cc'ing my school address so it doesn't get buried on my end.

Love,
Alex

subject: Poster dimensions and setup time
body:
Hello,

Thanks for accepting my abstract for the poster session. Two logistics questions before I send it to print:

- What are the maximum poster board dimensions? I want to lay out at 36 x 48 in portrait but can adjust.
- What time can presenters set up on the morning of the session, and will pins or clips be provided?

Appreciate the help.

Best,
Alex Santos
Puget Sound Institute of Science

subject: Re: Spring tide-pool outreach dates
body:
Hi Lily,

Great — Sound Shores can definitely staff a table for the spring low-tide series. Looking at the minus tides, the strongest morning windows are the weekends around the new and full moons.

We can bring the touch-tank props, ID cards, and two trained volunteers per date. Could the aquarium cover parking passes again? I'll send our volunteer roster once dates are confirmed.

Thanks,
Alex Santos
Sound Shores

subject: Shift swap request — finals week
body:
Hi Denise,

Could I swap my Wednesday evening bookstore shift during finals week? I have a committee-meeting conflict that afternoon that's likely to run long.

Tyler said he can cover Wednesday if I take his Saturday opening shift in return — he's cc'd to confirm. Happy to do the textbook buyback desk either day. Let me know if that works.

Thank you,
Alex

subject: Re: Student note — revision timeline
body:
Dear Editor,

Thank you for the reviewers' comments on our student note. The requested revisions are manageable: adding the 2025 baseline to Figure 2, clarifying the QA/QC steps in the methods, and expanding the limitations paragraph.

With my advisor's schedule, I can return the revised manuscript and a point-by-point response within four weeks — by mid-March at the latest. Please let me know if you need it sooner.

Sincerely,
Alex Santos
